findings.summary: Withaferin A and related withanolides from Withania somnifera inhibit platelet thromboxane A2 synthesis and collagen-induced aggregation in vitro, an effect mechanistically additive with aspirin's COX-1 inhibition; case reports describe unexpected bleeding tendency in patients combining low-dose aspirin with Ashwagandha supplementation.
findings.citation: Sangwan et al., Phytother Res 2011; case series summarized

findings.summary: Animal studies show Withania somnifera root extract prolongs bleeding time and clotting time at doses equivalent to standard human supplemental use, suggesting a pharmacodynamic antiplatelet contribution that may amplify aspirin's antihaemostatic effect.

Ashwagandha has a long Ayurvedic tradition as a 'Rasayana' adaptogen and is generally considered safe at recommended doses; existing adverse-event databases contain no confirmed case reports of major haemorrhagic episodes attributable specifically to aspirin-ashwagandha co-administration at typical supplemental doses.
findings.citation: Pratte et al., J Altern Complement Med 2014 (safety review)
